name: Visibility Meter
description: A player-facing and AI-facing measure of how detectable the character is based on light, stance, movement, distance, cover, and enemy perception rules.
design_notes: Visibility meter should expose the same detection ingredients that AI actually uses. Define visibility_score_points, light samples, stance modifiers, movement visibility multipliers, cover volumes, distance falloff, and detection thresholds. The meter should help players plan stealth routes without revealing so much precision that tension disappears.
design_purpose: Make hidden detection math readable enough for players to trust stealth failures.
Connect stance, light, cover, movement, and AI sightlines into one practical signal.
Help designers tune stealth encounters using the same score that appears in debug tools.
player_fantasy: The player feels able to read how exposed they are and adjust posture, route, or timing before being detected.

parameters.name: visibility_score_points
parameters.description: Current exposure score after stance, light, cover, and distance modifiers.

parameters.name: light_sample_radius_units
parameters.description: Radius used to sample lighting around the character body.

parameters.name: movement_visibility_multiplier
parameters.description: Exposure modifier applied by walking, sprinting, crouching, or crawling.

parameters.name: cover_volume_modifier
parameters.description: Exposure reduction from authored cover or concealment volumes.

parameters.name: detection_threshold_points
parameters.description: Score where AI starts suspicion or direct detection.

parameters.name: awareness_ui_state
parameters.description: Player-facing display state derived from the exposure score.

edge_cases: Different enemies may see the player from different distances or angles while one global meter is shown.
The player can be hidden from sight but loud enough to trigger hearing, requiring separate noise messaging.
Camera occlusion can make the player feel covered even when AI vision rays have a clear line.
Checkpoint restore should not show a stale detected state after AI perception has reset.
common_bugs: The UI meter uses player camera exposure while AI uses world light samples.
Movement visibility multiplier remains applied after sprinting stops.
Cover volumes overlap and multiply into near-zero visibility when only the strongest modifier was intended.
Detection threshold differs between UI and AI blackboard data, causing false trust.
balancing_notes: Precise meters support fair stealth but can reduce uncertainty; vague meters preserve tension but need strong feedback on failure.
Visibility scoring must be tuned with level lighting and patrol sightlines, not only character stance.
Enemy-specific meters are more accurate but can overwhelm players in crowded encounters.
accessibility_notes: Use text, icons, or shape changes alongside color to represent hidden, watched, and detected states.
Allow meter size and contrast adjustments because stealth relies heavily on reading this signal.

implementation_notes.unity: Compute visibility_score_points from shared visibility components before AI sight decisions and UI rendering. Use body probes, cover_volume_id, stance state, and enemy distance; debug views should show each contribution and the final detection threshold.
implementation_notes.godot: Store visibility data on a player stealth profile Resource and update it on a fixed perception tick. Area cover volumes, light probes, and movement_state_id feed the score before AI agents read it.
implementation_notes.unreal: Use Blackboard or replicated stealth components for visibility score, cover modifier, and UI state. AI sight decorators should read server-owned values rather than client display settings.
implementation_notes.web: Represent visibility as deterministic simulation data, not CSS or render brightness. Compute exposure from light zones, cover volumes, stance, and listener-specific distance before writing awareness UI state.

example_games.title: Thief: The Dark Project
example_games.note: Uses a light-based visibility indicator for stealth readability.

example_games.title: Mark of the Ninja
example_games.note: Uses clear visual stealth information to show detection risk.
